Patch Set 1:

This is the only patch to fail lustre-rsync-test test_5b in the past 4 weeks.  The only thing in the dmesg logs is on the client:

[37561.243928] LustreError: 28780:0:(mdc_changelog.c:230:chlg_load()) lustre-MDT0000-mdc-ffff88006872e800: fail to process llog: rc = -5

Can you please look into the kernel debug logs and see if this failure is related to the patch, or if a new LU ticket should be opened.

Patch Set 5:

Have you found any issues from the previous version of patch? Otherwise it's known to not pass racer.
Patch Set 5: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 5: Code-Review+2

I also added this comment in the ticket so it's not lost. not sure if we want a new feature-like ticket created for this?

I think another desired thing would be to more selectively drop bits from trylocks.

For example if we see a granted lock that blocks us, OR a waiting lock that blocks up based on non-optional bits, reducing our bits (or bits of that waiting lock) is pointless - because we know we would conflict no matter what.
Change has been successfully cherry-picked as 916bfe080520bdfb9a8d4e35ce7095c5b632f4eb by Oleg Drokin

parents.subject: LU-8626 hsm: expose the number of active hsm requests per type

commit.subject: LU-10269 ldlm: allow trybits in waiting queue
commit.message:
LU-10269 ldlm: allow trybits in waiting queue

Lock trybits can be kept while lock is waiting and each new
lock filters trybits of locks in the waiting queue. When lock
if granted finally remaining trybits are added to the granted
bits. Therefore trybits can be granted for blocking lock if
no other locks take these bits while lock is waiting.

Test-Parameters: mdscount=1 mdtcount=1 testlist=racer,racer,racer,racer
Signed-off-by: Mikhal Pershin <mike.pershin@intel.com>
Change-Id: I775f776f4cf8b581e32e4a1585e862e1764b5bed
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/30343
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Jinshan Xiong <jinshan.xiong@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
